---
title: "Projects"
subtitle: "The work I spend the most time on. Each links to a longer write-up."
toc: false
---

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[West Nile virus thesis — California mosquito surveillance](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd){.card-title}

A quantitative MPH thesis modeling WNV infection rate in *Culex* mosquito pools
against environmental covariates and surveillance effort, with spatial and
temporal structure handled explicitly on a county-month grid. In progress; no
results claimed yet.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[MESA — Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas](projects/mesa.qmd){.card-title}

A C++ desktop application for public-health mapping where data is thin:
geocoding, Getis-Ord Gi\* and Moran's I, and Monte Carlo permutation testing,
with the statistics validated against PySAL and R.

[Spatial statistics]{.tag} [C++]{.tag} [Desktop software]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[FEMA Hazus — GIS and risk-modeling software](projects/hazus.qmd){.card-title}

Software in support of FEMA's Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation program:
GIS-driven hazard and risk workflows, spatial data engineering, database
updates, and validation. Kept high-level and sanitized.

[GIS]{.tag} [Risk modeling]{.tag} [C#/C++]{.tag} [SQL Server]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[SEUHD practicum — applied public-health GIS](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd){.card-title}

Practicum with a local health department: respiratory-illness spatial analysis
with space-time cubes and emerging hot-spot analysis, plus immunization-
compliance reporting from EHR data and geocoded addresses.

[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

:::
